questions.question: Why interoperability in IoT is an issue?

options.a: loT nodes are heterogeneous hence they communicate in different protocols
options.b: IoT nodes are homogeneous and communicate with the same protocol

questions.explanation: Interoperability is a challenge because IoT systems combine diverse devices and platforms, each using different communication protocols[cite: 911]. This heterogeneity makes seamless data exchange difficult without standardized interfaces[cite: 912].

questions.question: UNSPSC provides a solution for which of the following?
questions.explanation: UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code) is an open, global standard for the efficient and flexible classification of products and services[cite: 923, 925].

questions.question: Arduino development boards are equipped with micro-controller processors which are ____ with respect to their hardware configurations.
questions.explanation: Arduino is an open-source based electronic programmable board[cite: 937]. Its hardware configurations and software (IDE) are publicly available for modification and distribution[cite: 942].

questions.question: Translation of inter-device communication forms an important part in solving device interoperability.
questions.explanation: Translation between different communication protocols is essential for enabling diverse IoT devices to work together[cite: 961]. Tools like UMB Adaptors translate local middleware messages into global metadata to solve this[cite: 953].

questions.question: With respect to the different wireless communication protocols such as Zigbee, Bluetooth, GPRS, 6LoWPAN and WiFi, which of the following terms can be associated with?
questions.explanation: Heterogeneity in IoT refers to the variety of different wireless communication protocols (ZigBee, Bluetooth, GPRS, etc.), hardware platforms, and programming languages[cite: 970, 971, 975].

questions.question: Which among the following are valid Arduino datatypes?
questions.explanation: Arduino supports various data types including void, long, int, char, boolean, unsigned char, and byte[cite: 985, 987, 991, 993].

questions.question: What is the purpose of the delay() function in Arduino programming?

options.d: To make the program go to sleep for a certain duration.

questions.explanation: The delay() function is a time manipulation function used to provide a pause of a specified duration in milliseconds[cite: 1018, 1019]. It halts operations temporarily for timing control[cite: 1020].

questions.question: Consider the following Arduino code snippet: String str = "Hello World"; String ptr = str.ToUpperCase(); What will be the value of String ptr?
questions.explanation: The ToUpperCase() function changes all the characters of a string to upper case[cite: 1035]. For "Hello World", this results in "HELLOWORLD"[cite: 1039].

questions.question: What does the following code snippet do in interfacing a servo motor with the Arduino MEGA board? int servoPin = 12;

options.a: Declares pin for connecting servo motor
options.b: Declares pin for providing power to MEGA board
options.c: Declares pin for Ground supply for servo motor

questions.explanation: This line of code declares an integer variable that represents the digital pin number on the Arduino board where the servo motor control wire is connected[cite: 1062].

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "The "Verify" option in the Arduino IDE checks the code for compilation errors."
questions.explanation: The "Verify" function in the Arduino IDE specifically checks the code for compilation errors[cite: 1068]. It compiles the code to find syntax or library issues without uploading it[cite: 1077].

questions.question: The tool used to select a particular COM port for connecting Arduino to a serial connector is called a sketch.
questions.explanation: A program coded in the Arduino IDE is called a sketch[cite: 1083]. The COM port is selected through the Tools menu, not the sketch itself[cite: 1087].

questions.question: In general, with respect to any sensor that can be connected to an Arduino board, which of the following is correct?

options.a: The sensor has infinite number of connecting pins
options.b: The sensor will have at least 3 pins (1+Vcc, 1 GND and 1 Data pin)
options.c: The sensor need not connect to the Arduino board.
options.d: The sensor will always have exactly 5 pins.

questions.explanation: Most basic sensors require at least three pins: power supply (+Vcc), ground (GND), and a data pin[cite: 1104]. For example, a DHT sensor uses pins for power, data, and ground[cite: 1099, 1100, 1102].

questions.question: You connect the +Vcc PIN of a sensor with which of the corresponding PIN of Arduino board.
questions.explanation: The +Vcc pin of a sensor is connected to a power supply pin on the board, such as the 3.3V (3V) pin[cite: 1113, 1114, 1120].

questions.question: Servo is a type of ____.
questions.explanation: A servo motor is listed as a type of motor actuator[cite: 1127, 1128]. It converts electrical signals into physical movement[cite: 1136].

questions.question: While uploading a sketch to an Arduino board, which of the following should be checked?
questions.explanation: Before uploading, the user must set both the board type and the correct serial port through the Tools menu[cite: 1148, 1149, 1150, 1151].
